inline_comments.message: now you are not catching exception at all. you only catch IOException in serializeResult and then do exit(-1). what if you get JsonSyntaxException from extractVLM. you are not failing the process or log it

now you are not catching exception at all. you only catch IOException in serializeResult and then do exit(-1). what if you get JsonSyntaxException from extractVLM. you are not failing the process or log it

In my opinion this way of terminating the program is too abrupt. What about other running threads? I can think, for example, of asych writing to the log, in which case the message about may never be written (depending on the implementation). I might be wrong, but something really bad must happen to justify System.exit() from deep within a program. On the other hand, System.exit can and should be used in main() to make the program shell-script-friendly. In this case an exit status can be checked if the program prints out/writes to log and doesn't throw an exception from its main().
